gate_question: Does this exact current protocol, with current live state, allow an unprivileged attacker to extract funds or rewards beyond entitlement?

candidate.title_or_claim: Reentrancy during bonding-curve-to-DEX migration allows attacker to buy at pre-migration bonding-curve price
candidate.claimed_root_cause: No nonReentrant modifier on swapExactInput; _getSwapImplementation reads live storage status; if PORTAL_TRADE_V2 calls external migrator before writing _setTokenStatus(DEX), a reentrant swapExactInput reads stale Tradable status and routes to PORTAL_TRADE_V2 again
candidate.claimed_impact: Attacker receives launched tokens at bonding-curve price after migration threshold is crossed, extracting value from the protocol reserve

live_preconditions.name: portal_trade_v2_source_ordering_of_setTokenStatus_vs_external_migrator_call
live_preconditions.observed_value: UNVERIFIABLE — PORTAL_TRADE_V2 source not present in Kirstennew/Portal--001; no PortalTradeV2.sol or equivalent found in src/; glob search returned only IPortalTradeV2.json ABI artifact
live_preconditions.command_if_missing: Decompile or obtain verified source for PORTAL_TRADE_V2 implementation address on BSC; check whether _setTokenStatus(DEX) sstore precedes or follows the external migrator call in the buy/migration code path

source_code_basis.why_it_matters: Entry point has no nonReentrant modifier; only guard is onlyIfBitFlagsSet(CB_BIT_MASK_GLOBAL_SWITCH); delegates to implementation returned by _getSwapImplementation

Reads live storage status via _getTokenState; returns PORTAL_TRADE_V2 if status==Tradable, PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER if status==DEX; this is a routing check not a reentrancy lock; stale Tradable read during reentrant call would route to PORTAL_TRADE_V2
Plain assembly sstore with no lock semantics; if called after external migrator callback, reentrant call reads pre-write Tradable status

grep for nonReentrant|ReentrancyGuard|_ENTERED returns zero hits in Portal's own source files; only matches are in lib/openzeppelin-contracts[-upgradeable] which Portal does not inherit for reentrancy protection

CRITICAL MISSING PIECE: the ordering of _setTokenStatus(DEX) vs external migrator call is entirely inside PORTAL_TRADE_V2 which has no source in this repository; _setTokenStatus(DEX) and LaunchedToDEX/dexSupplyThresh logic are absent from PortalBase.sol grep results, confirming they live in the external implementation

attacker_path.actor: unprivileged EOA controlling a contract with UniswapV3MintCallback or ERC-20 transfer hook
attacker_inputs: ExactInputParams with inputToken=quote token, outputToken=target token near dexSupplyThresh
amountIn sized to push circulatingSupply to dexSupplyThresh
malicious migrator callback or ERC-20 transfer hook that re-enters swapExactInput
call_sequence: 4. PORTAL_TRADE_V2 triggers migration: calls external migrator (IF _setTokenStatus(DEX) not yet written)
6. _getSwapImplementation reads status=Tradable (stale) -> routes to PORTAL_TRADE_V2 again
7. reentrant buy executes at bonding-curve price; attacker receives tokens
8. outer call resumes, _setTokenStatus(DEX) is written, liquidity added to DEX pool
state_after: attacker holds tokens purchased at bonding-curve price post-threshold

extraction_analysis.asset_or_reward: launched tokens purchased at bonding-curve price after DEX migration threshold is crossed
extraction_analysis.attacker_gain: tokens at below-market bonding-curve price; corresponding BNB/quote-token reserve drained from Portal
extraction_analysis.victim_or_protocol_loss: Portal proxy BNB reserve (1361+ BNB live) and/or quote-token reserve reduced by reentrant buy amount without valid post-migration entitlement
extraction_analysis.why_gain_exceeds_entitlement: after dexSupplyThresh is crossed the bonding-curve is closed; no further bonding-curve buys are entitled; reentrant buy at bonding-curve price is outside entitlement

setup: Identify a live token near dexSupplyThresh using getTokenV8Safe on the proxy
Deploy MockMigrator that re-enters swapExactInput on its callback

If PORTAL_TRADE_V2 source is unavailable, deploy a mock PORTAL_TRADE_V2 that: (a) increments circulatingSupply to dexSupplyThresh, (b) calls MockMigrator, (c) writes _setTokenStatus(DEX) only after migrator returns
Fund attacker with sufficient quote token / BNB
transaction_sequence: MockMigrator.migrate() re-enters portal.swapExactInput with small amountIn
expected_assertions: IF VULNERABLE: reentrant swapExactInput succeeds; attackerTokenBalance > 0 after reentrant call; status == Tradable at time of reentrant buy
IF PROTECTED: reentrant call reverts with TokenNotTradable or DEX-router failure on unfunded pool
local_proof_required.reject_if_assertion_fails: If reentrant swapExactInput reverts in all tested orderings, REJECT — the status write precedes the external call in the real PORTAL_TRADE_V2 and the routing check is sufficient

rejection_reason: Cannot promote to HIGH_CONFIDENCE_CANDIDATE because the single load-bearing assumption — that PORTAL_TRADE_V2 calls the external migrator BEFORE writing _setTokenStatus(DEX) — is unverifiable from this repository. PORTAL_TRADE_V2 source is absent from Kirstennew/Portal--001 (glob and grep confirm no PortalTradeV2.sol; _setTokenStatus(DEX) and dexSupplyThresh logic are absent from PortalBase.sol, confirming they reside entirely in the external implementation). If the real PORTAL_TRADE_V2 writes _setTokenStatus(DEX) before the external migrator call, the reentrant swapExactInput is routed to PORTAL_DEX_ROUTER and fails on an unfunded pool, collapsing the attack. The absence of a nonReentrant modifier and the plain-storage nature of _setTokenStatus are confirmed, but those facts alone do not establish exploitability without the ordering proof.
